question: Old Hask is running a 300-meter backbone link between two buildings at Ravenshade Academy. Which transmission medium is the best fit for that distance at gigabit speeds or higher?
feedback: Copper Ethernet is limited to about 100 meters per run. Fiber easily handles 300+ meters; multimode is common up to a few hundred meters, while single-mode is used for longer runs.

question: Which of the following correctly describes the difference between single-mode and multimode fiber?
correct: Single-mode uses a narrow core and a laser light source and is optimized for long distances
incorrect: Multimode uses a narrower core than single-mode and supports longer distances
Single-mode is typically orange or aqua; multimode is typically yellow
Both modes are limited to roughly 100 meters per run
feedback: Single-mode fiber has a ~9 micron core and uses a laser to carry one mode of light over long distances. Multimode has a larger core (50 or 62.5 micron), uses LEDs or VCSELs, and is used for shorter runs inside data centers.

question: Which cable type is required for runs installed in air-handling spaces such as drop ceilings because it produces less toxic smoke when it burns?
feedback: Plenum-rated cable uses a jacket (often FEP) that emits less toxic smoke and is required by fire codes in plenum spaces. Riser-rated cable is for vertical runs between floors but is not rated for plenum spaces.

question: Which of the following statements about Direct Attach Copper (DAC) cables are accurate?
correct: DAC cables are typically used for short runs inside a data center rack or between adjacent racks
DAC cables use twinaxial copper conductors with transceivers attached at each end
DAC cables are generally cheaper and lower-latency than an equivalent fiber optic link
incorrect: DAC cables are commonly used for runs longer than 100 meters
DAC cables are a form of single-mode fiber
DAC cables require a separate SFP transceiver to be purchased and inserted at each end
feedback: DAC is a short-reach, low-cost copper option with integrated transceivers on each end. It is popular for top-of-rack switch-to-server and switch-to-switch links of a few meters.

question: Which connector is typically used on RG-6 coaxial cable for cable-TV and cable-internet drops?
feedback: F-type threaded connectors terminate RG-6/RG-59 coax used in residential cable service. BNC is found on older 10BASE2 Ethernet and test equipment. RJ45 is for twisted-pair Ethernet, RJ11 for telephone.

question: Match each connector to the cable type it is used with.

pairs.definition: A small-footprint fiber connector commonly used in SFP transceivers
A larger square push-pull fiber connector
A multi-fiber push-on connector used for high-density parallel optics like 40/100G
An 8-position connector used on twisted-pair Ethernet cabling
A bayonet-style connector used on coaxial cable and test equipment

feedback: Recognizing connectors on sight and by name is a common Network+ objective. LC and MPO dominate modern data center fiber.

question: The small, hot-swappable transceiver form factor that plugs into a switch port and typically carries a single 1 Gbps or 10 Gbps channel is called a ___.
feedback: Small Form-factor Pluggable (SFP) supports 1 Gbps; SFP+ supports 10 Gbps. Higher-density and faster transceivers like QSFP, QSFP+, and QSFP28 carry 4 parallel channels.

question: Which 802.11 wireless standard operates ONLY in the 5 GHz band and is not backward compatible with 2.4 GHz-only devices?
feedback: 802.11a is 5 GHz only. 802.11b and 802.11g use 2.4 GHz. 802.11n is dual-band (2.4 and 5 GHz).

question: Which of the following are true about cellular and satellite WAN technologies?
correct: Satellite links typically have higher latency than terrestrial cellular links due to the signal distance
Cellular connectivity (LTE, 5G) is commonly used as a backup WAN link for branch offices
Satellite is often used in remote areas where wired or cellular service is unavailable
incorrect: Geostationary satellite links have lower latency than fiber internet
Cellular and satellite both require a clear line of sight to a nearby wireless LAN controller
5G cellular is limited to speeds below 10 Mbps under all conditions
feedback: Satellite is high-latency due to orbital distance (especially GEO). Cellular is an increasingly common WAN backup option, and 5G can offer high throughput.

question: In a star (hub-and-spoke) topology, how do endpoint devices communicate with each other?
correct: All traffic between endpoints passes through a central device such as a switch or hub
incorrect: Each endpoint is directly cabled to every other endpoint
Endpoints pass tokens in a closed ring to coordinate communication
Traffic is broadcast over a shared coaxial bus
feedback: The star topology centralizes all communication through a hub or switch. This is the dominant physical topology for modern Ethernet LANs.

question: Captain Doran is designing a data center network where every leaf switch connects to every spine switch, and east-west server-to-server traffic is a dominant pattern. Which architecture is he using?
feedback: Spine-and-leaf provides uniform, low-hop-count paths between any two leaves and scales east-west traffic well. Three-tier designs were optimized for north-south traffic flows.

question: Which of the following correctly describe the three-tier hierarchical network model?
correct: The access layer is where end-user devices connect to the network
The distribution layer aggregates access layer switches and enforces many policies
The core layer is optimized for fast, reliable backbone transport between distribution blocks
incorrect: The core layer is where individual end-user PCs connect
In a three-tier design, the access layer performs wire-speed routing between distribution blocks
A collapsed-core design always uses four separate tiers instead of three
feedback: Access = user connectivity, Distribution = policy and aggregation, Core = high-speed backbone. A collapsed core combines core and distribution into one tier for smaller networks.

question: Which term describes traffic flowing between servers inside the same data center, such as between an application server and a database server?
feedback: East-west traffic stays within the data center. North-south traffic moves between the data center and external clients or the internet.

question: Which of the following IPv4 ranges are reserved by RFC 1918 for private use?
feedback: RFC 1918 defines 10/8, 172.16/12, and 192.168/16 as private. 169.254/16 is APIPA (link-local), 127/8 is loopback, and 224/4 is multicast (Class D).

question: A Windows workstation that fails to contact a DHCP server assigns itself an address in the 169.254.0.0/16 range. What is this behavior called?
feedback: APIPA is a link-local fallback mechanism in the 169.254.0.0/16 range. It lets hosts on the same segment communicate when DHCP is unavailable, but has no default gateway.

question: The IPv4 address that always refers to the local host itself is ___.
feedback: 127.0.0.1 is the IPv4 loopback address, also reachable via the hostname 'localhost'. The entire 127.0.0.0/8 block is reserved for loopback.

question: Which IPv4 address class was historically defined for multicast traffic?
feedback: Class D (224.0.0.0 to 239.255.255.255) is reserved for multicast. Class E (240.0.0.0 and up) is reserved for experimental use.

question: An IPv4 address written as 192.0.2.10/24 uses ___ notation to indicate that the first 24 bits are the network portion.
feedback: CIDR notation replaces classful boundaries with an explicit prefix length, letting networks be subnetted on any bit boundary.

question: Which addressing technique lets a network administrator use different subnet mask lengths within the same larger network to size subnets to their actual host counts?
feedback: VLSM lets the same parent network be carved into subnets of different sizes (e.g., /30 for point-to-point links and /24 for user LANs) so address space is not wasted.

question: Which of the following are true about Software-Defined Networking (SDN) and SD-WAN?
correct: SDN separates control from forwarding and uses a central controller
SD-WAN can use MPLS, broadband, and LTE as underlays
SD-WAN often steers traffic based on the application in use
incorrect: SDN requires every switch to compute forwarding alone with no central coordination
SD-WAN can only run on dedicated MPLS circuits and not broadband or LTE
Zero-touch provisioning means every device must be manually configured on site before shipping
feedback: SDN centralizes control. SD-WAN extends that idea to WAN links and is transport-agnostic, application-aware, and supports zero-touch provisioning for remote deployment.

question: Zinna is designing a large data center where she needs to extend Layer 2 segments across a Layer 3 underlay and span multiple sites. Which technology is she most likely using?
feedback: VXLAN encapsulates Layer 2 frames inside UDP packets, allowing Layer 2 segments to ride over a Layer 3 underlay. It is widely used for data center interconnect (DCI).

question: Which of the following are core principles of a Zero Trust Architecture?
correct: Never trust, always verify — every access request is authenticated and authorized
Enforce least-privilege access so users and devices only get the minimum rights needed
Apply policy-based authentication that considers user, device, and context
incorrect: Any device connected behind the corporate firewall is implicitly trusted
Authorization decisions are made once at login and never re-evaluated
Internal network segments do not need to authenticate traffic
feedback: Zero Trust removes the idea of an implicitly trusted internal network. Every request is authenticated, authorized, and evaluated against policy — regardless of where it originates.

question: Which framework combines networking (SD-WAN) and security services (SWG, CASB, ZTNA, FWaaS) into a cloud-delivered model?
feedback: SASE converges WAN and security functions into a single cloud-delivered service. SSE is the security-only subset of SASE.

question: Which of the following statements about Infrastructure as Code (IaC) are correct?
correct: IaC uses version-controlled files to define infrastructure state
IaC helps detect and remediate configuration drift
Keeping IaC in source control enables branching and conflict review
incorrect: IaC expects administrators to log into devices and make manual changes directly
IaC tools cannot be used to perform bulk upgrades across many devices
Dynamic inventories must be maintained as static spreadsheets in IaC workflows
feedback: IaC treats infrastructure the same way developers treat code: version-controlled, reviewable, automatable, and auditable. Drift detection and dynamic inventories are common capabilities.

question: A primary motivation for deploying IPv6 is to mitigate IPv4 ___ exhaustion.
feedback: The global pool of IPv4 addresses is exhausted. IPv6's 128-bit address space (vs. IPv4's 32 bits) is the long-term solution.

question: Which IPv6 transition mechanism runs IPv4 and IPv6 on the same interface simultaneously, letting a host communicate over whichever protocol the destination supports?
feedback: Dual stack runs both protocols in parallel. NAT64 translates between IPv6-only clients and IPv4-only servers. Tunneling (e.g., 6to4) encapsulates IPv6 inside IPv4.

question: Which IPv6 transition technique allows an IPv6-only client to reach an IPv4-only server by translating between the two protocols?
feedback: NAT64 translates IPv6 packets to IPv4 and vice versa, typically paired with DNS64, which synthesizes AAAA records from A records.

question: Match each IPv4 address block to what it is used for.

pairs.definition: RFC 1918 private address space, large Class A block
APIPA link-local range assigned when DHCP fails
Loopback range; 127.0.0.1 refers to the local host itself
Class D multicast range used for one-to-many delivery

feedback: Recognizing these reserved ranges on sight is essential for Network+ troubleshooting scenarios.

question: Match each network topology or architecture to its best description.

pairs.definition: Every node has a direct link to every other node, providing maximum redundancy
All remote sites connect back to a single central hub site
Two-tier data center fabric where every leaf connects to every spine
A dedicated link directly connecting exactly two endpoints
A design that combines the core and distribution layers into a single tier

feedback: These topologies differ in redundancy, cost, and the traffic patterns they optimize for.

question: Brom is terminating fiber patch cables for a new 10 Gbps switch uplink that uses SFP+ transceivers. Which fiber connector is he most likely to install?
feedback: SFP/SFP+ transceivers almost always use the small LC duplex connector. MPO is reserved for higher-density parallel optics (40/100G), and ST is an older bayonet-style connector.
